goal_analysis: User wants me to read PLAN.md, identify which tasks/milestones are still unfinished, and produce an implementation plan to complete them. The blocker: PLAN.md has no per-task status markers, the README claims all milestones M0-M9 are already done and e2e-verified, the Go backend compiles with routes for every feature, yet there is a large uncommitted frontend refactor in progress and no active Jonggrang tasks. What counts as 'unfinished' is therefore ambiguous.

questions.question: PLAN.md milestones M0-M9 are all present in code and the README says they're done & e2e-verified. What is the actual gap you want this plan to close?
questions.rationale: Determines the entire scope and direction of the plan; 'unfinished' means very different things depending on this.

options.label: Finish the in-progress frontend refactor
options.rationale: There is ~1,629 lines of uncommitted web changes; this treats that WIP as the unfinished task and plans to complete + verify + commit it.

options.label: Audit PLAN.md milestone-by-milestone for real gaps
options.rationale: Distrust the 'done' claim; systematically verify each milestone's features actually work and plan to fill whatever is stubbed, broken, or missing.

options.label: New scope beyond current parity
options.rationale: Everything in PLAN.md is truly done; plan should cover new features/improvements not in the original plan (needs you to specify them).

options.label: Hardening, tests, docs & release polish
options.rationale: Features are done but production-readiness (test coverage, security, install.sh/goreleaser, docs) needs completing.

questions.question: How should the plan treat the uncommitted frontend changes (App.tsx, all pages, new components/ and lib/ dirs)?
questions.rationale: This WIP may itself be the 'unfinished task' or unrelated churn that should be committed/reverted separately before planning.

options.label: Build on / complete this WIP
options.rationale: Plan assumes this refactor is intended and incomplete; tasks will finish and validate it.

options.label: It's finished — commit it, then plan other work
options.rationale: The refactor is complete; plan focuses elsewhere and treats this as ready to commit.

options.label: Ignore / out of scope
options.rationale: This churn is unrelated to what you want planned; exclude it from the plan entirely.

questions.question: What does 'selesai' (done) require for verification in the plan?
questions.rationale: Decides how verification-heavy the plan is and whether a live orcinus cluster is assumed available.

options.label: Build + existing tests pass
options.rationale: Lightweight: compile, typecheck, run current test suite. Fast, no cluster needed.

options.label: Re-verify e2e against a live orcinus cluster
options.rationale: Heavier: plan includes real deploy/browser verification against an orcinus cluster (assumes one is reachable).
